title: Tender Tunisian Lamb Kebabs
description: A traditional Tunisian dish featuring lamb kebabs marinated in a mixture of muscovado sugar and spices, then grilled to perfection on a binchotan grill. The result is a charred and bouncy texture with an energizing flavor.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper to create a marinade.
Add the lamb cubes to the marinade and toss to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat the binchotan grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the grill with oil to prevent sticking, then grill the kebabs for 8-10 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the kebabs are grilling, mix the muscovado sugar with 1 tablespoon of water to create a glaze.
Brush the glaze onto the kebabs during the last 2 minutes of grilling.
Once the kebabs are cooked, remove them from the grill and let them rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with fresh herbs and a side of couscous or salad.
